title: SQL Injection via Untrusted Input
description: The system’s database interaction lacks explicit input validation and sanitization. An attacker can inject malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., search boxes, forms) to manipulate the database, potentially leading to data breaches, data modification, or even complete system takeover.
attack_vector: User input directly used in SQL queries without proper escaping or parameterized queries.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ input validation and sanitization to restrict acceptable input characters and formats.
Regularly update database software to patch known vulnerabilities.
